name: Prelude
description: Free-form character piece unified by a single mood, texture, or figuration pattern

sections.name: Opening / Establishment
sections.description: Establish the central figuration pattern and mood
sections.melody_character: Introduce the FIGURATION PATTERN - the defining textural idea of the prelude. This could be: a continuous arpeggiated pattern (like Chopin Op.28 No.1), a repeated rhythmic cell (like Bach WTC Prelude in C), a layered texture with melody emerging from figuration, or a single sustained mood. The pattern should be immediately distinctive and maintained throughout.
sections.accompaniment_character: The distinction between melody and accompaniment may be blurred - the figuration IS the piece. In some preludes, a melody floats above the pattern; in others, the pattern itself contains the melodic interest.
sections.harmonic_rhythm: Depends on character - can be very slow (one chord for many bars) or moderate
sections.ends_with: Continuation - no strong cadence, flows into next section

sections.name: Intensification
sections.description: The central pattern continues but with harmonic and dynamic intensification
sections.key_relation: moves through related keys, building tension
sections.melody_character: SAME FIGURATION PATTERN continues but: register expands (wider range), harmonic tension increases (chromatic chords, diminished chords, secondary dominants), dynamic builds toward climax. If there is a melody, it becomes more passionate/intense. The pattern may be slightly varied (added notes, thicker texture) but must remain recognizable.
sections.accompaniment_character: Pattern maintained but enriched. May add inner voices or bass movement.
sections.ends_with: Climax point - the highest dynamic and/or registral peak of the piece

sections.name: Resolution / Closing
sections.description: Gradual release of tension, return to opening character
sections.melody_character: Pattern continues but simplifies. Tension dissolves. May recall the exact opening material. Final bars should have a sense of completion - arriving home.
sections.accompaniment_character: Thinning texture, return to simplicity of opening
sections.ends_with: Final tonic, the pattern may stop abruptly or dissolve gradually

composition_rules: UNITY is the primary principle - one figuration pattern should pervade the entire piece
The pattern can be varied but must always be recognizable
Preludes are typically shorter than other forms (1-4 minutes)
Harmonic variety provides the main source of contrast (not thematic contrast)
The piece should feel like a single continuous gesture or mood
Dynamic shape is typically: quiet opening -> build to climax -> resolution
Avoid introducing completely new material - everything should relate to the opening pattern
The figuration pattern should be idiomatic for the instrument (playable, comfortable)
